title: chilocarpus denudatus
intro: Chilocarpus denudatus, also known as the 'Buddha's Hand' or 'Fingered Citron', is a rare and unique species of citrus plant that is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Chilocarpus denudatus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. Chilocarpus denudatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
